concept: Resilient / Able to Recover / Springing Back
description: Able to withstand or recover quickly from difficult conditions. 'Thuroi' names the quality of adaptive strength—bending without breaking, recovering from setback, returning to wholeness after stress.
sound_symbolism: The word bends and springs back. 'Thu' /θu/ begins with breath under pressure, stress applied. Then 'ro' holds and returns, form maintained. Finally 'i' springs up, recovery complete.
grammatical_notes: Functions as an adjective: 'thuroi sila' (resilient community). Related to 'luwi' (flexible) and 'mureo' (stable)—resilience combines flexibility with underlying stability.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Resilient systems can absorb shocks and continue functioning—the key quality for sustainable communities facing change.
pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Resilience comes from non-attachment—when we don't cling rigidly, we can adapt and recover.
